advisory.title: Cosmos-SDK Cosmovisor component may be vulnerable to denial of service
advisory.description: Unsupported Cosmovisor versions before v1.0.0, distributed with Cosmos SDK releases before 0.46, could be driven into denial of service and, when automatic binary downloads were enabled, a potential remote code execution path. This advisory maps to HackerOne report 2094056, RCE and DoS in Cosmovisor, reported to the Cosmos Bug Bounty Program.

affectedPackages.name: Cosmovisor
affectedPackages.vulnerableRange: Cosmovisor < v1.0.0, distributed with Cosmos SDK < 0.46

timeline.notes: The original Cosmovisor merge introduced stdout scanning for UPGRADE NEEDED messages plus DAEMON_ALLOW_DOWNLOAD_BINARIES-driven fetching. The vulnerableHead is the cosmovisor/v0.1.0 release commit, which is in the advisory's affected < v1.0.0 range. The cosmovisor/v1.0.0 line rewrote the scanner around upgrade-info.json file watching and removed the built-code upgradeRegex/NEEDED-at stdout trigger.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should detect that Cosmovisor trusts upgrade instructions discovered through daemon output and can download and execute upgrade binaries when DAEMON_ALLOW_DOWNLOAD_BINARIES is enabled.

checks.name: hackerone_report_maps_to_advisory
checks.details: HackerOne report 2094056 is titled RCE and DoS in Cosmovisor, and GHSA-23px-mw2p-46qm is the Cosmos SDK repository advisory for Cosmovisor denial of service and potential RCE reported to the Cosmos Bug Bounty Program.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the cosmovisor/v0.1.0 release commit, and GHSA-23px-mw2p-46qm lists Cosmovisor < v1.0.0 / Cosmos SDK < 0.46 as affected.

checks.name: patched_release_removes_stdout_scanner
checks.details: cosmovisor/v1.0.0 scanner.go uses upgrade-info.json file watching, while the vulnerable release contains upgradeRegex matching UPGRADE NEEDED lines from daemon output.
